73968.subject: RE: Переклад.
73968.body:
Добрий день!
Можемо забезпечити переклади, але попередньо рекомендуємо проконсультуватись з церквою,  де буде проводитись обряд вінчання які документи необхідні для проведення цієї процедури.  

Адреса офісу:

 <https://www.google.com/maps/search/Torysk%C3%A1+3+040+11+Ko%C5%A1ice?entry=gmail&source=g> Toryská 3

 <https://www.google.com/maps/search/Torysk%C3%A1+3+040+11+Ko%C5%A1ice?entry=gmail&source=g> 040 11 Košice

2-й поверх, 110  кабінет

Робочі години – 8:00 – 16:00, пн-пт.

З повагою,

UA preklady s.r.o.

+421918635854





 

 

From: Oleg Novosadov <novosadovoleg@gmail.com> 
Sent: Tuesday, June 2, 2026 8:36 PM
To: info@ua-preklady.sk
Subject: Переклад.

 

Доброго дня, 

 

Цікавить переклад документів для вінчання у Словаччині.

 

Дякую. 

 

Гарного дня.

73959.subject: Ethereum's First Post-Quantum Migration Spec Lands
73959.body:
**gm, Defiers!**

* **[Ethereum researchers](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/ethereum-researchers-lay-out-post-quantum-key-registry-as-first-concrete-migration-step)**[ lay out XMSS registry, the first post-quantum fork step](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/ethereum-researchers-lay-out-post-quantum-key-registry-as-first-concrete-migration-step)

* **[Coinbase Ventures](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/coinbase-ventures-ena-open-market-ethena-distribution-deal)**[ buys ENA on the open market; Ethena distribution deal](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/coinbase-ventures-ena-open-market-ethena-distribution-deal)

* **[OpenSea](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/opensea-teases-hyperliquid-powered-perpetuals-launch)**[ teases Hyperliquid-powered perpetuals launch](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/opensea-teases-hyperliquid-powered-perpetuals-launch)

* **[Galaxy](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/galaxy-launches-institutional-otc-prediction-markets-desk-arca-clarity)**[ opens institutional OTC desk for Polymarket and Kalshi](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/galaxy-launches-institutional-otc-prediction-markets-desk-arca-clarity)

* **[MoneyGram](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/moneygram-launches-mgusd-stablecoin-on-stellar)**[ launches MGUSD stablecoin on Stellar with Bridge as minter](https://thedefiant.io/news/tradfi-and-fintech/moneygram-launches-mgusd-stablecoin-on-stellar)

* **[Trezor CTO](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/trezor-cto-interview-defi-yield-morpho-curation)**[ on DeFi yield strategies and Morpho-curation risk](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/trezor-cto-interview-defi-yield-morpho-curation)_[ [Partner]](https://thedefiant.io/news/defi/trezor-cto-interview-defi-yield-morpho-curation)_

Ethereum Foundation researchers **Thomas Coratger** and **Justin Drake** (author of the EF's [post-quantum roadmap](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/ethereum-researchers-lay-out-post-quantum-key-registry-as-first-concrete-migration-step)) published a draft spec for an XMSS public-key registry, the first piece of protocol plumbing the network would need to migrate validators off BLS signatures (the cryptographic primitive that backs validator attestations, which a sufficiently scaled quantum computer could break). XMSS is hash-based, stateful, and quantum-resistant.

This spec is relevant on its own, but** it’s also the third concrete output from inside the EF's narrowed mandate in two weeks**. On May 25, Vitalik [published a vision](https://thedefiant.io/newsletter/vitalik-outlines-a-leaner-ethereum-foundation) cutting the Foundation's scope down to core protocol research, client development, and public-goods funding. The same day, an EF-aligned working group [shipped the Kohaku wallet-privacy SDK](https://thedefiant.io/news/blockchains/ethereum-foundation-kohaku-sdk-privacy-wallet-integration-bb4t52), a library wallets can integrate for private transactions. Three days later, Vitalik [pu

🧠 AI’s Crypto News Brief: Institutional conviction is tested as a rare sale by MicroStrategy and geopolitical tensions sparked a $1 billion liquidation event. However, aggressive treasury buys by Strive and major tokenization milestones by Franklin Templeton signal underlying market maturity. 🚨MicroStrategy (Strategy) Sells First Bitcoin Since 2022, Sparking $1B Liquidations Michael Saylor's firm disclosed selling 32 BTC for $2.5M to fund preferred stock dividends, ending a multi-year accumulation streak. Compounded by US-Iran geopolitical friction, the move triggered a flash crash below $69,000 and liquidated over $1 billion in leveraged positions. Read full story 💰Strive Adds 2,500 BTC to Treasury, Becomes 7th Largest Public Holder Asset manager Strive (ASST) capitalized on the market dip to acquire an additional 2,500 Bitcoin at an average price of $74,092. The purchase brings its total holdings to 19,000 BTC, signaling strong institutional buy-side support amid retail panic. See details 🏛️Franklin Templeton Partners with MoonPay for On-Chain Fund Access The $1.7 trillion asset manager is integrating its Benji Technology Platform with MoonPay Trade to facilitate seamless swaps between stablecoins and tokenized money market funds. The partnership provides institutions with 24/7 liquidity and yield through fully regulated blockchain workflows. See details 🌍Ripple Launches RLUSD Stablecoin in Turkey for Institutional Use Ripple has partnered with local exchanges BiLira, Bitexen, and Bitlo to bring its USD-backed stablecoin, RLUSD, to the Turkish market. Targeting one of the world's most active crypto economies, the move facilitates institutional enterprise payments and inflation hedging. Read full story ⛓️Hamilton Lane’s HLSCOPE Fund Debuts on Tron Blockchain via Securitize In a major shift for the RWA sector, Hamilton Lane has launched its Senior Credit Opportunities Fund on the Tron network. This marks the first Securitize-issued asset on Tron, leveraging the chain's massive stablecoin liquidity for institutional private credit. See details 📈Backpack Launches Securities Platform for 24/7 Stock Trading on Solana Crypto exchange Backpack has introduced "Backpack Securities," a platform blending traditional brokerage with on-chain tokenization. Users can trade real U.S. stocks and convert them into tokenized assets f

73898.subject: Data & Insights: Funding, Futures, DEX Volume Are All Down
73898.body:
The Data and Insights newsletter brings the highlights of the week from The Block’s Data Dashboard

( https://www.theblock.co/data/crypto-markets/spot )

Gm data enjoyers, happy June! This week, we’re watching markets digest Microstrategy selling the first of its Bitcoin (STRC holders rejoice!). Our team recently went live with our crypto funding dashboard ( https://www.theblock.co/data/alternative-crypto-metrics/crypto-funding ) , with over 13,000 startups, data going back to 2014, and every private VC deal in crypto tracked! We recommend giving it a check.

This week, we’re taking a look at futures trading volume and funding updates. We’ll also look at Polymarkets US stats, Hyperliquid’s rising share against CEXs, Binance’s foray into stocks, and the state of onchain trading. So grab a drink, take a seat, and let's dive in.

1: Future(s)-Proof

( https://www.theblock.co/data/crypto-markets/futures/total-futures-volumes )

Chart via The Block's Data Dashboard

*Key Insights* provided by The Block Research

* Total futures volume across major exchanges hit a 12-month low in May, closing the month at approximately $2.9 trillion, a level not seen since late 2023 and well below the $6-7 trillion monthly peaks recorded during last year's more active trading periods.

* The decline reflects a broader pullback in speculative activity across crypto markets, with spot volumes and on-chain activity similarly subdued heading into the summer.

* The composition of that volume remains heavily concentrated among a handful of exchanges, with Binance maintaining its dominant share, followed by OKX, Bybit, and Gate. Smaller venues have seen the most pronounced erosion in activity as traders consolidate onto deeper liquidity pools during quieter periods.
* Against this backdrop, the CFTC moved ( https://www.theblock.co/post/403040/cftc-opens-door-for-crypto-perpetual-future-contracts-in-us-as-coinbase-kalshi-move-forward ) to formally open the door for crypto perpetual futures contracts in the US, a structurally significant development for a derivatives product that has until now existed largely outside the reach of US regulation.
* Perpetual futures differ meaningfully from traditional futures instruments: they carry no expiry date, eliminating rollover costs and calendar risk. Funding rates, paid periodically between longs and shorts, continuously anchor the contract price to spot, functioning as a real-time gauge of market sentiment and leverage positioning. Combined with capital efficiency advantages over margin accounts, perps have become the dominant trading vehicle in crypto derivatives globally.

* The regulatory opening matters less for access, as a meaningful share of US-based traders already route through offshore exchanges via VPNs, and more for what legitimized domestic perp markets could unlock via institutional participation, cleaner compliance infrastructure, and onshore liquidity that does not require regulatory arbitrage.
